decisions.reasoning: Holding 9 shares entered today at $218.96. NVIDIA reports Q1 FY2027 earnings after close tomorrow (May 20), with consensus expecting $79.2B revenue (+79.5% YoY) and EPS of $1.78 (+120% YoY). 37 analysts rate Buy with $276.95 consensus PT; Morgan Stanley raised to $285, Wells Fargo to $315. All SMA signals long. Position ~19.7% of equity. Not trimming—size is manageable and structural AI demand thesis intact.

sources.headline: Nvidia Earnings: Live Updates and Commentary May 2026
Wells Fargo Hikes NVIDIA Price Target to $315 Ahead of Earnings
NVIDIA Falls 4% on Rotation Concerns: How Strong Profitability Could Save the Bull Case

decisions.invalidation: Guidance disappoints on China-ex revenue or hyperscaler capex signals softening; stock closes below $200 post-earnings

decisions.reasoning: Holding 3 shares as core broad-market exposure. S&P 500 closed April at fresh ATH 7,209 with Q1 earnings growth 15.1% (sixth consecutive double-digit quarter). Real GDP +2.0% annualized Q1, unemployment 4.3%. Analyst year-end targets raised to 7,900-8,250. Elevated forward P/E 20.9x warrants no additional size but thesis supports holding.

sources.headline: May 2026 Economic and Market Update: New Highs and Old Risks

decisions.invalidation: Forward P/E compression, Fed policy reversal, or recession signals; GDP growth turns negative

decisions.reasoning: Initiated 5-share position at $425.31. MSFT consolidated after March 2026 sell-off (low ~$357); recovered 19% from lows. Azure growing 40% YoY, AI revenue run rate $37B, RPO backlog grew ~100% per Wedbush (May 13, $575 PT). 55 of 58 analysts rate Buy, average PT $560.63 (+32% upside). Near-term capex/OpenAI pressure is well-known and reflected in March trough. No upcoming earnings binary.

sources.headline: Analysts See 41% Upside for Microsoft Stock After Nearly 100% Backlog Growth
Wedbush sets $575 price target for Microsoft

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth decelerates below 30% or OpenAI losses accelerate beyond expectations; stock closes below $400

summary: First session: held NVDA (earnings tomorrow) and SPY, added MSFT on Azure/AI thesis; day P/L -$11.52 on broad market softness
open_theses: NVDA: holding through Q1 FY27 earnings (May 20 AH); beat + strong guidance could push toward $276-315 analyst targets
MSFT: Azure 40% growth + AI monetization; capex headwind priced in after March sell-off, mean-reversion toward $560 target range
SPY: broad market core; watching elevated P/E 20.9x and AI concentration risk
